source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Two singly ionized atoms move out of a slit at point S in figure and into a magnetic field of magnitude \( 0.100 \, \mathrm{T} \) pointing into the page. Each has a speed of \( 1.00 \times 10^6 \, \mathrm{m/s} \). The nucleus of the first atom contains one proton and has a mass of \( 1.67 \times 10^{-27} \, \mathrm{kg} \), whereas the nucleus of the second atom contains a proton and a neutron and has a mass of \( 3.34 \times 10^{-27} \, \mathrm{kg} \). Atoms with the same number of protons in the nucleus but different masses are called isotopes. The two isotopes here are hydrogen and deuterium. Find their distance of separation when they strike a photographic plate at \( P \).

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'in the figure' and refers to spatial points S and P
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the visual setup is important
the separation at the photographic plate depends on the trajectory geometry shown in the figure, which is not fully specified in text alone
asset_registry_record shows an image exists and registry_passed=true
multi_step: solving requires using magnetic-force circular-motion reasoning rather than direct lookup
the problem gives two different isotope masses, so the solver must compute or compare two radii
the final separation requires combining the two trajectory results with the plate geometry shown in the figure
multiple physical quantities are provided (charge state, magnetic field, speed, masses), consistent with a multi-step calculation
verifiability: answer_type is short_text with a single numeric result ('0.210 m')
multiple-choice options are provided, giving an additional consistency check
the quantities needed for computation are explicitly stated in the text, making rule-based or numerical verification feasible
asset registry passed and both question and answer are present

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present, and asset registry passed with no reported issues
image dependency is high, and the required figure is available
multi-step and verifiability scores are strong, indicating good collection value and answer checkability
